prompt.text: A cheetah is ___ (fast) than a rhino.

answers.text: faster

items.hintDe: Kurzes Adjektiv (eine Silbe): häng einfach -er an die Grundform.
items.explainDe: Bei einsilbigen Adjektiven bildest du den Komparativ mit -er: fast → faster. Kein more davor.
items.note: SB ex. 8 facts: cheetah is the fastest land animal; rhino tops out at 55 km/h. Both unit animals.

prompt.text: A giraffe is ___ (tall) than an ostrich.

answers.text: taller

items.hintDe: tall hat eine Silbe – einfach -er anhängen.
items.explainDe: tall → taller. Einsilbige Adjektive bekommen nur -er, niemals more.
items.note: From the Animal Quiz "Which is the tallest? giraffe / ostrich / elephant" (SB ex. 13).

prompt.text: An anaconda is ___ (heavy) than a crocodile.

answers.text: heavier

items.hintDe: heavy endet auf -y: das -y wird zu -ier.
items.explainDe: Bei Adjektiven auf -y wird das y zu -ier: heavy → heavier. Nicht more heavy.
items.note: Anaconda & crocodile are unit reptiles; fact-file ex. 9 weighs snakes.

prompt.text: An elephant is ___ (big) than a rhino.

answers.text: bigger

items.hintDe: big: den Endkonsonanten verdoppeln, dann -er: bigger.
items.explainDe: Bei big verdoppelst du das g: big → bigger. Ein einzelnes g (biger) ist falsch.
items.note: WB ex. 6 model "Elephants are bigger than rhinos."

prompt.text: A dolphin is ___ (intelligent) than a pigeon.

answers.text: more intelligent

items.hintDe: intelligent hat vier Silben – nimm more davor, nicht -er.
items.explainDe: Lange Adjektive (3+ Silben) steigerst du mit more + Adjektiv: more intelligent. Kein -er.
items.note: From Animal Quiz "Which is the most intelligent? dolphin / pig / chimpanzee" (SB ex. 13).

prompt.text: A rabbit is bad at climbing, but a pig is ___ (bad) than a rabbit.

answers.text: worse

items.hintDe: bad ist unregelmäßig: bad → worse. Auswendig lernen!
items.explainDe: bad hat eine unregelmäßige Steigerung: bad → worse. Nicht badder und nicht more bad.
items.note: SB box exception bad → worse; "he's worse than me" pattern, animal-themed (rabbit & pig in-bank).

prompt.text: My rat is ___ (hairy) than your dog, and it is ___ (small) too.

answers.text: hairier | smaller

items.hintDe: Erst hairy (y → -ier), dann small (nur -er). Trenne deine Antworten in der richtigen Reihenfolge.
items.explainDe: hairy → hairier (y wird zu -ier), small → smaller (nur -er). Beide sind kurze Adjektive ohne more.
items.note: Two-blank guided production; "My pet rat has got lots of fur. It's very hairy." (MORE Words ex. 4); rat is g1-u09.

prompt.text: Look at a dog and a mouse.

answers.text: A dog is bigger than a mouse.

distractors: A dog is more bigger than a mouse.
A dog is biger than a mouse.
A dog is big than a mouse.
items.hintDe: Such die richtige Form von big: -er, ein doppeltes g, und kein zweites Steigerungswort.
items.explainDe: Richtig ist bigger than: big verdoppelt das g und braucht kein more. Doppelte Steigerung (more bigger) ist immer falsch.

distractorPool: A dog is more big than a mouse.
A dog is the bigger than a mouse.
A dog is more bigger than a mouse.
A dog is biger than a mouse.
A dog is big than a mouse.

items.note: Pet-comparison frame from SB ex. 5 speaking task; dog & mouse in-bank.

prompt.text: It is a cold, wet morning.

answers.text: The weather is worse today than yesterday.

distractors: The weather is more bad today than yesterday.
The weather is more worse today than yesterday.
The weather is the worse today than yesterday.
items.hintDe: bad → worse ist unregelmäßig. Es gibt kein worser und kein more davor.
items.explainDe: Die Steigerung von bad ist worse – ohne extra Endung und ohne more. more worse gibt es nicht.

distractorPool: The weather is more bad today than yesterday.
The weather is more worse today than yesterday.
The weather is badder today than yesterday.
The weather is the worse today than yesterday.
The weather is the most bad today than yesterday.

items.note: Irregular worse; weather frame fully in-bank (g1).

prompt.text: A cheetah's speed is 110. A rhino's speed is 55.

answers.text: The cheetah is faster than the rhino.

distractors: The cheetah is more fast than the rhino.
The cheetah is faster as the rhino.
The cheetah is more faster than the rhino.
items.hintDe: Vergleich mit than, und fast bekommt nur -er.
items.explainDe: Beim Vergleich brauchst du than (nicht as): faster than. fast bekommt nur -er, kein more.

distractorPool: The cheetah is more fast than the rhino.
The cheetah is faster as the rhino.
The cheetah is more faster than the rhino.
The cheetah is fast than the rhino.
The cheetah is the faster than the rhino.

items.note: Speeds (cheetah 110; western black rhino up to 55) from SB facts ex. 6 + ex. 8; 'km/h' dropped (tokenises to out-of-bank km+h), 'speed' is g2-u04 vocab. Bare numbers pass.

prompt.text: A cheetah is more fast than a tortoise.

answers.text: A cheetah is faster than a tortoise.
faster

items.hintDe: fast ist kurz – kein more. Nur -er anhängen.
items.explainDe: Kurze Adjektive nehmen kein more: aus more fast wird faster.
items.note: SB common-error "A lion is more fast than a turtle" → unit/in-bank animals (tortoise is g1-u09).

prompt.text: A giraffe is more bigger than a chimpanzee.

answers.text: A giraffe is bigger than a chimpanzee.
bigger

items.hintDe: Doppelte Steigerung! Entweder more oder -er, nie beides.
items.explainDe: more bigger ist doppelt gesteigert. Richtig ist nur bigger – mit -er und ohne more.
items.note: SB common-error double comparative; unit animals.

prompt.text: An ostrich is more good at running than a pigeon.

answers.text: An ostrich is better at running than a pigeon.
better

items.hintDe: good ist unregelmäßig: good → better. Kein more good.
items.explainDe: Die Steigerung von good ist better. more good gibt es nicht – good → better musst du auswendig lernen.
items.note: verify r1 (answers): reversed subjects — ostriches ARE famous runners, so 'a pigeon is better at running than an ostrich' was false; 'an ostrich is better than a pigeon' is true. The good→better error under correction is unchanged.

prompt.text: An elephant is heavy. A mouse is light. (heavy) An elephant is ___.

answers.text: heavier than a mouse
an elephant is heavier than a mouse

items.hintDe: heavy endet auf -y: heavy → heavier, dann than.
items.explainDe: heavy → heavier (y wird zu -ier) + than: heavier than a mouse.
items.note: SB box example "An elephant is heavier than a mouse." light/heavy both in-bank.

prompt.text: Maths is difficult. English is not. (difficult) Maths is ___.

answers.text: more difficult than English
maths is more difficult than English

items.hintDe: difficult hat drei Silben → more difficult than.
items.explainDe: Lange Adjektive steigerst du mit more: more difficult than. Nicht difficulter.
items.note: Long-adjective more + than; school subjects are g2-u01 vocab.

prompt.text: Mein Hund ist größer als deine Maus.

answers.text: My dog is bigger than your mouse.
My dog is bigger than your mouse

items.hintDe: groß → big → bigger (g verdoppeln) + than.
items.explainDe: big → bigger (Endkonsonant verdoppeln) und als = than: bigger than your mouse.
items.note: Standard comparative translation; 'cat' is out-of-bank so swapped to 'mouse'/'Maus' (g1-u09).

prompt.text: Ein Wal ist schwerer als ein Delfin.

answers.text: A whale is heavier than a dolphin.
A whale is heavier than a dolphin

items.hintDe: schwer → heavy → heavier (y wird -ier) + than.
items.explainDe: heavy → heavier und als = than: A whale is heavier than a dolphin.
items.note: Adapts SB box "The whale is bigger than a dolphin" to heavy; whale & dolphin are unit animals.

prompt.text: is / a / shark / than / dolphin / a / more dangerous

answers.text: A shark is more dangerous than a dolphin.
A shark is more dangerous than a dolphin

items.hintDe: Wer? + is + more dangerous + than + Wer? more und dangerous gehören zusammen.
items.explainDe: Reihenfolge: A shark is more dangerous than a dolphin. dangerous ist lang, also more dangerous.

distractorPool: as
the
is
than

items.note: Sharks & dolphins are unit sea-animals; long-adjective ordering. 7 answer tokens + 3 distractor chips = 10.

prompt.text: big, bigger, biggest

items.hintDe: Kurze Adjektive: -er. Lange: more. good und bad sind unregelmäßig.
items.explainDe: big → bigger, happy → happier, dangerous → more dangerous. good → better und bad → worse sind Ausnahmen.
items.note: Form-drill covering all three patterns (regular -er, -y→-ier, more, irregular).

prompt.text: Which is bigger?

items.hintDe: Eine Silbe → -er. Drei oder mehr Silben → more.
items.explainDe: Kurze Adjektive (fast, tall …) nehmen -er. Lange Adjektive (dangerous, intelligent …) nehmen more.
items.note: Concept sort: -er adjectives vs more adjectives, all unit-relevant describing words.

prompt.text: An antelope is ___ than an elephant.

answers.text: faster

items.hintDe: fast + die Endung für den Komparativ.
items.explainDe: fast → faster: einsilbiges Adjektiv plus -er.
items.note: Single-word comparative form drill; English carrier (anagram answer is the one word 'faster'). Antelope faster than elephant — true and in-bank.

prompt.text: An elephant is ___ than a mouse.

answers.text: heavier

items.hintDe: heavy endet auf -y – denk an die Schreibänderung.
items.explainDe: heavy → heavier: das -y wird zu -ier.
items.note: Spelling-change comparative (y→ier); answer is the single word 'heavier'. SB box "An elephant is heavier than a mouse."

prompt.text: a rhino / heavy / a hippo

answers.text: Is a rhino heavier than a hippo?
Is a rhino heavier than a hippo

items.hintDe: Frage mit Is am Anfang: Is ... heavier than ...?
items.explainDe: Komparativ-Frage: Is a rhino heavier than a hippo? – is an den Anfang, heavy → heavier, dann than.
items.note: Mirrors SB ex. 5 guessing game ("Is it a guinea pig?"); rhino & hippo in-bank.

prompt.text: A mosquito is ___ (small) than a bat.

answers.text: smaller

items.hintDe: small ist kurz: nur -er anhängen.
items.explainDe: small → smaller. Kurzes Adjektiv, nur -er, kein more.

distractorPool: more small
smallest
more smaller
the smaller
small than

items.note: Mosquito & bat are unit animals; bumblebee bat is the smallest mammal (SB ex. 8).

prompt.text: The cheetah is ___ (fast) land mammal.

answers.text: the fastest

items.hintDe: Superlativ: the + Adjektiv + -est.
items.explainDe: fast → the fastest. Beim Superlativ steht immer the davor und -est am Ende.
items.note: SB ex. 8 fact "The fastest land animal ... is the CHEETAH" — 'animal' swapped to in-bank 'land mammal'.

prompt.text: The whale is ___ (heavy) mammal of all.

answers.text: the heaviest

items.hintDe: heavy endet auf -y: -y wird zu -iest, und the davor.
items.explainDe: heavy → the heaviest. Das -y wird zu -iest, davor steht the.
items.note: SB ex. 8: "... It's also the heaviest." 'on land or in the sea' → 'of all' ('sea' is out-of-bank); whale is heaviest mammal.

prompt.text: A crocodile can be ___ (long) of all crocodiles.

answers.text: the longest

items.hintDe: long ist kurz: the longest.
items.explainDe: long → the longest. Einsilbiges Adjektiv, the + -est, kein most.
items.note: SB ex. 8: estuarine crocodiles are the longest crocodiles; 'reptile' is out-of-bank, so head noun is 'crocodile(s)'.

prompt.text: The mosquito is ___ (dangerous) for people of all.

answers.text: the most dangerous

items.hintDe: dangerous hat drei Silben: the most dangerous.
items.explainDe: Lange Adjektive: the most + Adjektiv. the most dangerous – nicht dangerousest.
items.note: SB ex. 8: "The most dangerous animal ... is the MOSQUITO." Mosquito is an insect, so worded 'most dangerous for people of all' (not 'mammal'). 'malaria'/'illness' dropped (both out-of-bank).

prompt.text: I think the dolphin is ___ (intelligent) mammal of all.

answers.text: the most intelligent

items.hintDe: intelligent ist lang: the most intelligent.
items.explainDe: the most intelligent: lange Adjektive nehmen the most, nicht -est.
items.note: Animal Quiz ex. 13 "Which is the most intelligent?"; 'of all' replaces out-of-bank 'in the sea/water'.

prompt.text: A pig is bad at climbing, but a hippo is ___ (bad) at it.

answers.text: the worst

items.hintDe: bad ist unregelmäßig: bad → the worst.
items.explainDe: Der Superlativ von bad ist the worst. Nicht the baddest und nicht the most bad.
items.note: Irregular bad→the worst; carrier in-bank and animal-themed (climbing passes).

prompt.text: Sam runs faster than anyone in the class.

answers.text: Sam is the fastest runner in our class.

distractors: Sam is the most fastest runner in our class.
Sam is the faster runner in our class.
Sam is fastest runner in our class.
items.hintDe: Superlativ braucht the + -est: the fastest. Kein most davor.
items.explainDe: Richtig ist the fastest runner: the + -est, ohne most. Und das the darf nie fehlen.

distractorPool: Sam is the most fastest runner in our class.
Sam is the faster runner in our class.
Sam is fastest runner in our class.
Sam is more fast runner in our class.
Sam is the most fast runner in our class.

items.note: Class-context superlative; double superlative + missing-the distractors. 'Anna' is out-of-bank so swapped to 'Sam' (harvested proper noun).

prompt.text: No mammal is more powerful than the whale.

answers.text: The whale is the most powerful mammal of all.

distractors: The whale is the powerfulest mammal of all.
The whale is most powerful mammal of all.
The whale is the more powerful mammal of all.
items.hintDe: powerful ist lang: the most powerful. the darf nicht fehlen.
items.explainDe: Lange Adjektive: the most powerful. Nicht powerfulest, und the muss dabei sein.

distractorPool: The whale is the powerfulest mammal of all.
The whale is most powerful mammal of all.
The whale is the more powerful mammal of all.
The whale is the most powerfulest mammal of all.
The whale is powerful mammal of all.

items.note: Long-adjective the most + missing-the/over-regularised distractors; whale is a unit sea-mammal ('of all' avoids out-of-bank locatives).

prompt.text: The giraffe is taller than the ostrich and the elephant.

answers.text: The giraffe is the tallest.

distractors: The giraffe is the most tall.
The giraffe is the most tallest.
items.hintDe: the + tall + -est = the tallest. the nicht vergessen, kein most.
items.explainDe: the tallest: the + -est. Ohne the (tallest) oder mit most (the most tall) ist es falsch.

distractorPool: The giraffe is tallest.
The giraffe is the most tall.
The giraffe is the most tallest.
The giraffe is the taller.
The giraffe is more tall.

items.note: Animal Quiz "Which is the tallest?" (SB ex. 13).

prompt.text: The whale is the most biggest mammal.

answers.text: The whale is the biggest mammal.
the biggest

items.hintDe: Doppelter Superlativ! Entweder most oder -est, nie beides.
items.explainDe: the most biggest ist doppelt. Richtig ist the biggest – nur -est, ohne most.
items.note: SB common-error double superlative; whale is biggest mammal (SB ex. 8).

prompt.text: The cheetah is fastest land mammal.

answers.text: The cheetah is the fastest land mammal.
the fastest

items.hintDe: Vor dem Superlativ fehlt the.
items.explainDe: Beim Superlativ muss the davor: the fastest land mammal.
items.note: SB common-error missing 'the'; SB ex. 8 cheetah fact.

prompt.text: This is the most good book about mammals I have read.

answers.text: This is the best book about mammals I have read.
the best

items.hintDe: good ist unregelmäßig: good → the best. Kein the most good.
items.explainDe: Der Superlativ von good ist the best. the most good gibt es nicht.
items.note: Irregular good→the best; "best book I have read" pattern, mammals-themed.

prompt.text: The bat is small. No mammal is smaller. (small) The bat is ___.

answers.text: the smallest mammal
the bat is the smallest mammal
the smallest

items.hintDe: small → the smallest. the + -est.
items.explainDe: small → the smallest mammal: the + -est. Kein most.
items.note: SB ex. 8: "The BUMBLEBEE BAT ... is the smallest mammal in the world." (bat = unit animal; 'bumblebee' modifier dropped as out-of-bank).

prompt.text: Der Wal ist das größte Säugetier.

answers.text: The whale is the biggest mammal.
The whale is the biggest mammal

items.hintDe: größte → big → the biggest (g verdoppeln). Säugetier = mammal.
items.explainDe: big → the biggest (Endkonsonant verdoppeln), mit the davor: the biggest mammal.
items.note: SB ex. 8 fact translated; Säugetier = mammal (in-bank head noun).

prompt.text: Mathe ist das schwierigste Fach.

answers.text: Maths is the most difficult subject.
Maths is the most difficult subject

items.hintDe: schwierig hat ein langes englisches Adjektiv: the most difficult.
items.explainDe: difficult ist lang: the most difficult subject. Nicht difficultest.
items.note: Long-adjective the most; subjects are g2-u01 vocab.

prompt.text: the / is / cheetah / land mammal / the / fastest

answers.text: The cheetah is the fastest land mammal.
The cheetah is the fastest land mammal

items.hintDe: the + cheetah + is + the fastest + land mammal. most brauchst du nicht.
items.explainDe: The cheetah is the fastest land mammal. fast nimmt -est und braucht kein most.

distractorPool: most
more
as
than

items.note: SB ex. 8 cheetah fact, scrambled; 'most' chip traps double superlative. 6 answer tokens (land mammal = 2) + 1 distractor = 7 ≤ 12.

prompt.text: big and the biggest

items.hintDe: Kurz: the + -est. Lang: the most. good und bad sind unregelmäßig.
items.explainDe: big → the biggest, happy → the happiest, beautiful → the most beautiful. good → the best und bad → the worst sind Ausnahmen.
items.note: Form-drill across all three superlative patterns + irregulars.

prompt.text: Which is the biggest?

items.hintDe: Kurze Adjektive → the …-est. Lange → the most …
items.explainDe: tall → the tallest, heavy → the heaviest. Lange Adjektive wie dangerous → the most dangerous.
items.note: Concept sort: -est adjectives vs most adjectives, unit describing words.

prompt.text: The whale is the ___ mammal.

answers.text: heaviest

items.hintDe: heavy endet auf -y – denk an die Schreibänderung beim Superlativ.
items.explainDe: heavy → heaviest: das -y wird zu -iest.
items.note: Single-word superlative spelling change (y→iest); answer is 'heaviest'. SB ex. 8 whale fact. 'the' is in the carrier so the anagram word is just 'heaviest'.

prompt.text: which / tall / a giraffe / an ostrich / an elephant

answers.text: Which is the tallest?
Which is the tallest
Which is the tallest, a giraffe, an ostrich or an elephant?

items.hintDe: Quizfrage: Which is the + Superlativ? tall → the tallest.
items.explainDe: Which is the tallest? – die typische Quizfrage aus dem Buch. the + -est.
items.note: Reconstructs the Animal Quiz header "Which is the tallest?" (SB ex. 13).

prompt.text: The anaconda is one of ___ (long) of all.

answers.text: the longest

items.hintDe: long → the longest.
items.explainDe: long → the longest: the + -est, kein most.
items.note: WB ex. 11 "The anaconda is one of the longest snakes" → 'one of the longest of all' ('snakes'/'reptile' both out-of-bank).

prompt.text: A chimpanzee is ___ a child. They are both clever.

answers.text: as clever as

items.hintDe: Gleichheit: as + Adjektiv + as. Beide Wörter sind as.
items.explainDe: Wenn etwas gleich ist, nimmst du as ... as: as clever as. Nicht so ... as und nicht as ... like.
items.note: SB ex. 10 Sounds Right "he's a chimpanzee, and he's as big as me"; clever is unit adjective (ex. 4). child is g1.

prompt.text: The boy is ___ the chimpanzee. They are both very big.

answers.text: as big as

items.hintDe: Gleich groß → as big as.
items.explainDe: as big as: zwischen den beiden as steht die Grundform big, nicht bigger.
items.note: SB ex. 10: "he's as big as me." boy is g1.

prompt.text: An anaconda is ___ a crocodile. They are both very long.

answers.text: as long as

items.hintDe: Gleich lang → as long as. Grundform long, kein longer.
items.explainDe: as long as: die Grundform long steht zwischen as und as, nicht longer.
items.note: Anaconda & crocodile are unit reptiles, both long animals.

prompt.text: An antelope is fast, but it is ___ a cheetah. The cheetah is faster.

answers.text: not as fast as

items.hintDe: Ungleich → not as ... as. Das Erste hat weniger davon.
items.explainDe: not as fast as bedeutet: das Erste ist weniger schnell. not so fast as ist falsch.
items.note: Brief seed pattern "not as fast as a cheetah"; antelope & cheetah are both 'fast' quiz options (SB ex. 13). leopard out-of-bank → antelope.

prompt.text: A dolphin is ___ a shark. The shark is more dangerous.

answers.text: not as dangerous as

items.hintDe: not as dangerous as – die Grundform dangerous, ohne more.
items.explainDe: not as dangerous as: zwischen as und as steht dangerous ohne more, weil as ... as keinen Komparativ braucht.
items.note: WB ex. 7 "... isn't as dangerous as the mosquito" pattern; sharks & dolphins are unit sea-animals.

prompt.text: A pigeon is ___ a parrot. They are both quite small.

answers.text: as small as

items.hintDe: Gleich klein → as small as.
items.explainDe: as small as: Grundform small zwischen den beiden as.
items.note: SB box "It was as small as a mouse" pattern; pigeon & parrot are unit birds.

prompt.text: A chimpanzee and a dolphin are both clever.

answers.text: A chimpanzee is as clever as a dolphin.

distractors: A chimpanzee is so clever as a dolphin.
A chimpanzee is as cleverer as a dolphin.
A chimpanzee is as clever like a dolphin.
items.hintDe: Such die richtige as ... as Form: beide Male as, Grundform dazwischen.
items.explainDe: Richtig ist as clever as: zwei Mal as und die Grundform clever. so ... as, ...cleverer... und ...like sind falsch.

distractorPool: A chimpanzee is so clever as a dolphin.
A chimpanzee is as cleverer as a dolphin.
A chimpanzee is as clever like a dolphin.
A chimpanzee is as clever than a dolphin.
A chimpanzee is clever as a dolphin.

items.note: Chimpanzee & dolphin = the two 'most intelligent' quiz animals (SB ex. 13).

prompt.text: An elephant weighs much more than an antelope.

answers.text: An antelope is not as heavy as an elephant.

distractors: An antelope is not so heavy as an elephant.
An antelope is as not heavy as an elephant.
An antelope is not as heavier as an elephant.
items.hintDe: not as heavy as: Grundform heavy, kein heavier, und die Wortstellung not as ... as.
items.explainDe: not as heavy as: not + as + Grundform + as. Nicht so ... as, nicht heavier, und das not steht vorne.

distractorPool: An antelope is not so heavy as an elephant.
An antelope is as not heavy as an elephant.
An antelope is not as heavier as an elephant.
An antelope is not as heavy like an elephant.
An antelope is not heavy as an elephant.

items.note: WB ex. 7 "Elephants aren't as heavy as blue whales" pattern; antelope & elephant are unit mammals.

prompt.text: You and a friend look at two rats. They are both very big.

answers.text: My rat is as big as your rat.

distractors: My rat is so big as your rat.
My rat is as bigger as your rat.
My rat is as big like your rat.
items.hintDe: Gleich groß → as big as. Beide Male as.
items.explainDe: as big as: zwei Mal as, Grundform big dazwischen. so ... as und as ... like sind L1-Fehler.

distractorPool: My rat is so big as your rat.
My rat is as bigger as your rat.
My rat is as big like your rat.
My rat is as big than your rat.
My rat is big as your rat.

items.note: Rats are in-bank (g1-u09); equality context forces as ... as.

prompt.text: An anaconda is so long as a crocodile.

answers.text: An anaconda is as long as a crocodile.
as long as

items.hintDe: Nicht so ... as, sondern as ... as.
items.explainDe: Im Englischen sagt man as ... as, nicht so ... as: as long as.
items.note: verify r1 (answers): swapped to a TRUE equality — an ostrich is NOT as tall as a giraffe (false; the unit even glosses the giraffe as the tallest mammal), but an anaconda and a crocodile are comparably long. The so→as error under correction is unchanged.

prompt.text: A rhino is as stronger as a hippo.

answers.text: A rhino is as strong as a hippo.
as strong as

items.hintDe: Zwischen as ... as steht die Grundform: strong, nicht stronger.
items.explainDe: Bei as ... as bleibt das Adjektiv in der Grundform: as strong as, nicht as stronger as.
items.note: SB common-error comparative inside as...as; rhino & hippo are in-bank (strong is unit adjective).

prompt.text: A dolphin is as smart like a chimpanzee.

answers.text: A dolphin is as smart as a chimpanzee.
as smart as

items.hintDe: Das zweite Wort ist as, nicht like.
items.explainDe: Beide Male steht as: as smart as. like ist hier falsch.
items.note: SB common-error like→as; smart is unit adjective, both animals are 'intelligent' quiz options.

prompt.text: A dog is faster than a tortoise. (not as ... as) A tortoise is ___.

answers.text: not as fast as a dog
a tortoise is not as fast as a dog

items.hintDe: langsamer = nicht so schnell: not as fast as.
items.explainDe: Wenn die Schildkröte langsamer ist, ist sie not as fast as der Hund – Grundform fast zwischen den beiden as.
items.note: Comparative→equality transformation from v1 seed; dog & tortoise in-bank.

prompt.text: Your room is smaller than my room. (not as ... as) Your room is ___.

answers.text: not as big as my room
your room is not as big as my room

items.hintDe: kleiner = nicht so groß: not as big as.
items.explainDe: smaller bedeutet not as big as: Grundform big zwischen den as.
items.note: verify r1 (answers): made the transform coherent — the source now compares the SAME subject across both halves ('Your room' vs 'my room'), removing the unsignalled pronoun remap that let a careful student be marked wrong.

prompt.text: Ich bin so groß wie mein Papa.

answers.text: I am as tall as my dad.
I am as tall as my dad
I'm as tall as my dad.
I'm as tall as my dad

items.hintDe: so ... wie = as ... as. groß bei Personen = tall.
items.explainDe: so groß wie heißt as tall as – beide Male as, Grundform tall dazwischen.
items.note: Standard as...as translation; 'brother/father' are out-of-bank so subject is 'I' and 'dad' (in-bank).

prompt.text: Ein Delfin ist nicht so gefährlich wie ein Hai.

answers.text: A dolphin is not as dangerous as a shark.
A dolphin is not as dangerous as a shark
A dolphin isn't as dangerous as a shark.
A dolphin isn't as dangerous as a shark

items.hintDe: nicht so ... wie = not as ... as.
items.explainDe: nicht so gefährlich wie heißt not as dangerous as – die Grundform dangerous bleibt.
items.note: not as...as translation; dolphin & shark are unit sea-animals; Hai = shark.

prompt.text: as / is / a / dolphin / as / a / chimpanzee / smart

answers.text: A dolphin is as smart as a chimpanzee.
A dolphin is as smart as a chimpanzee

items.hintDe: as + smart + as. Das Wort like brauchst du nicht.
items.explainDe: A dolphin is as smart as a chimpanzee. Beide Male as, niemals like.

distractorPool: like
so
than
more

items.note: Dolphin & chimpanzee are the two 'intelligent' quiz animals; 'like' chip traps the L1 error. 8 answer tokens + 1 distractor = 9 ≤ 12.

prompt.text: as ... as

pairs.left: A pigeon is not as big
An antelope is not as fast

items.hintDe: Jede Hälfte endet mit as + Tier. Achte auf den Sinn (gleich oder nicht gleich).
items.explainDe: Bei as ... as gehört nach dem Adjektiv immer as + das zweite Tier: as clever as a dolphin.
items.note: Sentence-half matching; all subjects unit animals.

prompt.text: a cheetah / fast / an antelope

answers.text: Is a cheetah as fast as an antelope?
Is a cheetah as fast as an antelope
Is an antelope as fast as a cheetah?

items.hintDe: Frage mit Is am Anfang: Is ... as fast as ...?
items.explainDe: as...as-Frage: Is a cheetah as fast as an antelope? – is vorne, Grundform fast zwischen den as.
items.note: Cheetah & antelope are both 'fast' quiz options (SB ex. 13).

prompt.text: How big? How fast?

groups.label: as … as
members: An anaconda is as long as a crocodile.
A chimpanzee is as clever as a dolphin.

groups.label: not as … as
members: An antelope is not as fast as a cheetah.
A dolphin is not as dangerous as a shark.

items.hintDe: as ... as = gleich. not as ... as = nicht gleich.
items.explainDe: as ... as heißt, beide sind gleich. not as ... as heißt, das Erste hat weniger davon.
items.note: Meaning sort (equal vs not equal); all sentences unit animals.

prompt.text: I am ___ my best friend. We are both quite strong.

answers.text: as strong as

items.hintDe: Gleich stark → as strong as.
items.explainDe: as strong as: Grundform strong zwischen den beiden as.
items.note: MORE Words: "I'm not as strong as my best friend." (strong example sentence) — here positive as...as.
